CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
I took some time to review Karmel's most up-to-date executive summary for the GLBBH grant application, after originally sending her the wrong attachment. Below is my response:


Hi Karmel,


I am so sorry for the confusion. Again, please feel free to delete the previous email that I sent you. Attached is your executive summary with some revisions made by me! Feel free to review. 

I think that you have some great content - it is very authentic to you and your business, and I think there is great value in that. You have the air of authority on your business operations, and your target customers. Great work in that area!

One focus I had was on the formatting: to get the text uniform and within the 5-page limit. I also made some suggestions with rephrased sentences or comments, highlighted in purple. 

If there are any other questions that you have for me, please feel free to let me know. I have availability any time after 10:30 tomorro

We discussed the recent results from local business traction from in person trial sales calls. The feedback has been quite positive with some follow up orders and referrals.. There is also an opportunity for sales through a social media influencer, with terms to be worked out. A BAF for legal services would be quite helpful to put in place employee agreements and a sales agreement.

I compiled an extensive competition research analysis and found some direct competitors having custom design and NFC/QR code capabilities, including one in Germany. Creating a unique AI generated landing page for small business customers will provide a unique aspect to the business, as envisioned by Carter. The competition research also uncovered some existing independent tools that could be integrated with the product, but need further analysis to determine best fit. We also walked through the NFC coding process to explore how to update it to unlock fresh content. On the internet a user could save

I had also looked at getting a 50k personal loan and down sizing my mill. But for some reason the under writing bump it down to 10k. Diane sent me the below email and called to express her frustration once again with the MSHDA My Neighborhood grant. Her last meeting went well, she recorded it and Tonya stated MSHDA would cover 75% of costs and her portion would be 25%. She received the below email which indicates they siding is only at 50%. Diane left a voice message requesting clarification on the change and was told to accept it today or they are closing her file. I crunched the numbers on the chart she sent me and it appears the roof is being covered at 81%, Windows at 75% and Siding at 50%.


I asked Diane if she is comfortable with signing the agreement and moving forward with this grant. I recommended she read the full contract, to make sure she is comfortable in signing and agreeing to the terms. She stated, she is going to move forward because according to the message she received from Tonya, they have tried to make this work, with all of the changes (many of them issues on their end)

From: Tammy R. Dupuis

Sent: Friday, May 23, 2025 12:52 PM

To: Don Dobler <dondobler@gmail.com>

Subject: LLC formation and due diligence checklist


Hi Don ,

Thanks for meeting with me today. I have attached

PREP: Compared the data sent by the current owner regarding revenue and costs from 2024 to the projections they have for 2025 and beyond. Reviewed written portion of plan in Live Plan and made a few minor suggestions for updates, but that was in great shape.


CRITICAL PROBLEMS:

  1. Comparing 2024 actual sales to the 2025 projections, there was a $200K difference. Several categories of revenue were drastically higher. Considering that they likely won't take possession until July or August, and the bulk of the sales are made in summer months, this was just too much of an increase. Part was due to categories that don't exist yet, like lotto and prepared hot foods; and part was from what they would be doing to increase other areas and add in new categories. Since this is unlikely to happen yet in 2025 and bankers prefer conservative projections, we went over each revenue section together and came up with more feasible 2025 figures.
  2. Gross margi

Zoom with Gavin Gardner, Michael Blicher and Anna Bier. Update on recent drone related events and demos. Discussed DOD SBIR application process for a direct to Phase II that is underway. Discussed roles of SBDC TT and bbc-etc, ETF application timing, and other business needs (funding, parts to build more drones, domestication of supply chain).

Summary/Action: Clay Phillips, Joshua Mehay, Michael Blicher and I had a phone call to discuss the SBIR/STTR programs in general: Brief History of the SBIR Program, SBIR Program Objectives, SBIR Eligibility, SBIR Three Phases, Brief History of the STTR Program, STTR Program Objectives, STTR Small Business Eligibility, STTR Non-Profit Institution Eligibility, STTR Three Phases, Advantages of SBIR/STTR Programs, Three Types of SBIR Projects, Contract Agencies, Grant Agencies, Research Topics and Agency Solicitations, Phase I success, Phase II success, Possible SBIR Timeline, Is SBIR/STTR right for the company? We went over the details of the program and how I can work with them. I told them that I am a high-energy physicist who had a small business in Virginia before coming to Michigan. The R&D of our particle accelerator research company was funded by the SBIR program and we had over 30 awards (phase I and II) in 8 of the federal agencies before selling off the business in 2001.

He is working on several rocket technologies aimed initially at low volume cube-sat launches. He submitted 11 SBIR applications and has 5 in queue. ClientIntake.BusinessType: Real Estate and Rental and Leasing

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: I met with our client by telephone to talk about her plans to purchase a multi-unit rental property to occupy one unit while renting out the others. She formed a company in February 2024 called Bougee Life, LLC. I noted that she needs to complete her Annual statement for 2025. Her plan is to take advantage of what she sees as a favorable market for rental properties and purchase a property or two that are on the market. I shared a copy of the Shareable Business Model with her to begin working on the business plan. I also sent her several resources such as the VIQ report for residential rental properties. I expect to be working with her on a couple of projects. I sent her this email: https://michigansbdcatgrandvalleystateuniv.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/EmailMessage/02sVs00000SCjsVIAT/view

Topic: Funding for a church project


Whitney continues to seek capital for start-up and money for the church steeple repair

  • The original roof contractor will not return her calls
  • Serenus Johnson bid came back at $16k for labor (she has materials)
  • She is going to ask producer if they can assist
  • Joe Frost from the MEDC visited the site to learn more, did not offer MEDC programs
  • Has received more sponsorships for other materials
  • Show is coming back to film soon
  • Has started on the business plan for the Parsonage (room revenue)
  • Will do a second business plan for the My Church Kitchen (chef dinners)
  • Has decided to go forward with GoFundMe
  • Realizes that storytelling will help her move the project forward

Advice and next steps

  • Sent partner release (received) and will connect Whitney to key contacts in Bay City that may mentor/give advice/possibly connect (done 5/27)

Tom gave me a call regarding the SEO report that we had worked on prior to the GLBBH grant application took over our attention. I attached the report, and gave him some background information for review:


Hi Tom,


As discussed, here is the SEO report that our SBDC Lead Center grad students worked on, auditing your website. 

As you review this material, please bear in mind that this report focuses on best practice advice for SEO data hygiene. We can review this report together if you would like, and I'll be happy to answer any specific questions or strategies you might want to put in play for your website. 

I hope that this report is helpful, and again, feel free to let me know if you would like to discuss it together!


Best,

Emily

Location & Space Development

  • Space Overview:

    • Restroom facilities are existing but should be inspected for upgrades or compliance if necessary.
  • Outdoor Area:
      • Space for smoker and possible outdoor seating or patio. 
    • Ensure proper infrastructure for outdoor cooking and safety regulations.

Client requested help growing business and with cash flow. Client scheduled meeting but did not show up. Sent follow up email

Quick recap

Lauren and Kelly discussed various aspects of setting up and running Lauren's cleaning business, including insurance coverage, website creation, and pricing strategies. They explored different pricing models and calculated potential earnings for different scenarios, while also discussing the costs associated with running the business such as taxes, salary, and supplies. The conversation concluded with recommendations for business setup, including setting up a business bank account, using QuickBooks for bookkeeping, and establishing proper invoicing procedures.

Business Insurance and Licensing Review

Lauren and Kelly discussed insurance for Lauren's business, confirming that the quote received was correct and suitable, with liability coverage of $500,000 per incident and $1 million annually, at a total annual cost of $882. They also clarified that business license posters were not necessary, as Lauren had already received her LLC license numb

Good Day!


I hope you're doing well! I wanted to check in and see how things are progressing with your business. If there’s any guidance or advice I can provide based on where y

Zoom call with Scott. HIs platform is focused on helping drive business decision making. He currently has about 178 test users for his product. In discussing his status, he struggled to articulate the pain points he is solving or being able to define his early adopter market or key value proposition. We discussed next steps to conduct additional customer discovery and better define these as well as how to refine his marketing messaging.

Jackie wants to open a dog kennel but needs a three year financial business plan for the bank. She wants a full plan for herself.

I set her up with a LivePlan. we will meet June 3rd to work on plan.


She wanted to know what to do about business insurance, I suggested she start with her home owners. Of they do not then look at another local place so she can know her agent.


She has already contacted the health department about her septic tank and the county about her zoning, both are set.


She has no expectation as to when the project will be done, she figures once the bank okays it.

Meeting looking at kitchen went well, but space did not have a stove all pizza oven things, owner does not want to remove the pizza oven or remodeling of the space so may not be a good fit? ...able to purchase a large commercial stove which will have to be vented. Will look for other space. Church $100 day. Maybe 2 days a week, meals only 2 days? Start slow. Establish a customer base and then look to expand? Business Model order from a menu, deliver? Can clients legally pick up at the church? OR Personal Chef Services? Where to deliver area? Do you charge? Best days of the week, Tuesday/Wed? and Holidays? Start of school year, fall/winter/spring? less summer? Continue to look at competition and pricing and volume/capacity delivery and/or pick up?

ClientIntake.BusinessType: Real Estate and Rental and Leasing

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
**CONSULTATION**


Met with Jasmine this week to discuss her barndominium-based real estate development concept. She is working on completing her business plan (still drafting the revenue section). We reviewed the grant list she shared. She’s aiming to purchase 15–30 acres and is exploring ways to increase value with fewer acres by maximizing square footage per unit. Jasmine connected with Katie from Market One, who helped schedule a June 5th meeting with Covert Township Supervisor Daywi Cook to discuss potential land options. We've scheduled two more sessions — one before and one after that meeting — to stay on track.

So many ideas- Client wants to open a Aerospace parts manufacturing business that accesses government contracting. She is currently a manager for a company doing government procurement and wants to be in business for herself.


Molly has talked to APex and found she needs to be established for two years before government contracts will work with her. She then said that the idea might be too big so she thought she could manage her fiancés webpage building business and look for government contracts that way. She asked if she was crazy. I suggested she research both. Talk to Apex about the webpage idea and do the research on manufacturing. I sent her VerticalIQ for both Machine Shops and Aircraft Engine & Parts Mfrs.


She talked to Chris Burger from Northern Wings in Newberry and got his card. I suggested she reach out to him after her research. She will book another appointment when she's ready.

[*HRS are cumulative (Uplift/TRYE Team)]

He is also actively working to have electric vehicle charging stations installed and eventually work with a realtor to potentially purchase a building that would house his businesses.


He switched from considering Nectar Juice Bar franchise to Pure Green. They have more items than the other and there is a couple in Rochester Hills that currently owns a franchise that he may be able to talk to. Start-up funds are $270K estimated, needing a space near 900 SF with 8 workers.


He is also speaking with a Konos North Shore franchise that does smoothies and food (Hawaiian McDonalds description.) with needs of 1,200 SF. Discussed the need to obtain detailed franchise information and, eventually, have legal review of the offerings. He said he also is considering s

Lance Wolfe <lwolfe@mtu.edu>

Client has parted ways with Steven the professor. It was a clean break so now Adam is able to pursue this company without limitations. He needs funding to get his manufacturing started. I recommended the different funding options and pros and cons of each.

Christie faced several challenges as she worked through the financial planning and fundraising strategy for her healthcare platform. She needed to refine her pricing model for platform subscriptions, integration fees, and nurse navigation services, while also ensuring her revenue projections, staffing plans, and operational costs were realistic. She wrestled with how to scale services based on patient volume and system size, how to staff efficiently at launch, and how to plan her budget for insurance, legal expenses, and office space. Additionally, Christie was rethinking her original two-phase funding approach, uncertain about whether she could justify selling a partial product during phase one and concerned about securing sufficient capital to support long-term growth.


I recommended that Christie consolidate her fundraising plan into a single $1.2 million raise rather than splitting it into multiple phases, allowing for a stronger launch and better market position

She feels Ready to start!

Hi Levi,

I hope you’re doing well and that you had a chance to relax a bit over the Memorial Day weekend. I meant to reach out Monday as promised, but I completely forgot it was the holiday—thank you for your patience.

It was great catching up with you last week. I’m glad to see how much progress you’ve made, especially setting up your LLC and EIN, creating your own business plan and projections, and testing your concept at the Kalamazoo Comic Con. Your proactive approach with reels, supplier connections, and even exploring property options is setting a solid foundation.

As discussed, I’m attaching a local industry data sheet focused on Book Stores and Hobby, Toy, and Game Stores in Kalamazoo County. This might help you get a better sense of the market landscape and inform updates to your business plan.

Next steps we discussed:

  • Start scouting for a potential property (preferably with a realtor).

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Russell called my cell phone stating he was ready to work on his financial analysis and loan package development. He and his wife have completed compiling all of their numbers for the past two years. I'm traveling to Reed City tomorrow so will only be within 20 minutes of his location. He offered a tour of his AFC before our meeting to work on his loan package and review of his numbers.


Plan: I will meet with them on Wednesday, May 28th at 11:30am.

Focus Four Meeting with David and Leandra. Update on her scorecard and core purpose statement. Leandra shared her Core Purpose and feels good about it. David advised not to rush it and that it is okay to not be in a hurry. He recommended she continue to share with the Team and get input. Keep it on her radar for the next 30 days and let it marinate. It sends a bad message if she begins promoting it and then changes it in 6 months, so better to take time with it. We discussed wrapping up the loose ends of Focus Four and David shared the following:

  1. Put the Core Values in a certain order: Reliability, Teamwork and Collaboration, Honesty, Respect, Creativity.
  2. Finalize Core Purpose - Get input from her team.
  3. Finalize her Scorecards (OPS & Draft 2025). Formulate what a goal should be. Determine what you want to share and don't want to share. Can take a year to get these right.
  4. Prioritize the weekly meeting: Use the suggested meeting cad

James

05/27/2025

5:30PM




Customer Discovery Discussion

Hi Adrian and Mariant,


The last time I spoke with Mariant, I mentioned that Adrian has been offering Customer Discovery Training through the Small Business Support HUB in partnership with Latinx. She has developed her own approach to understanding the needs of ideal customers and has some openings to schedule training sessions!


I think Mariant is at a point in developing her business where you both would gain significant value from a conversation. I know you are already very well-acquainted, but I wanted to send a short email with Adrian'

Met with Christie from Coreo, did follow-up review and feedback for pitch deck. Talked through parallel language structure and not making audience do too much thinking to connect dots in points of slides. Talked about element structure and language on slides, as well as conservative, judicious use of very basic animations (only appear and quick fade) only when necessary for the point. Talked through go-to-market and growth slides. Also gave homework to figure out smooth flow in pitch narrative, thinking through how each slide is a simple logical transition step from the one before it (i.e. each slide connects logically to the one before it and the one after), and using this to identify where slides overlap, loop back, or are redundant in the story). Set follow-up to continue going through slides.

Follow up email sent:

Subject: RE: Kileen -- Love-A-Bowls business plan


Hi Ephy,
Provided details on the BAF software development pilot criteria, and background for development services that are available for it.

James

05/27/2025

7:00PM




Market Research Insights On Operations And Management Consulting.

Hi Mariant,


I started doing some research in the Operations and Management Consulting industry and found some information you may find helpful. Due to the global atmosphere of business nowadays and the economic uncertainty small business owners are experiencing, they are looking for consultants who can help them build strategies for maximizing growth and achieving a return on their investment.


They are interested in both long-term and short-term solutions, as well as cutting down on waste and inefficient p

Media.Other: I found you on social media

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: Kitt wanted to add more products to her google merchant account. We downloaded the product list but the formatting was incorrect. We had to add sku numbers, but may not have done it correctly. She will research the SKU and we will try again next week.

Hey Liz, hope you’re doing great! One of our SBDC interns is going to be giving you a call— we’re going to highlight you and Blue Mystique Inn for our client success story! Let me know if you have any questions. Thanks, Nicole🙂


Texted Liz

Subject: Michigan SBDC check-in

 

Hi

Analysis

Andrea has recently reached a major milestone in her entrepreneurial journey. After completing the One and All program, she not only refined her new business idea centered around wellness, but also received personalized support that she found incredibly valuable. As a result of her dedication and progress, she was awarded the $15,000 Enhance Grant, which has already been deposited into her business account. This funding gives her a strong financial foundation to begin building her business with intention and strategy.

Although she’s facing a recent life change—losing her job—she’s using it as an opportunity to go all in. Before diving into her business full-time, she plans to spend a month doing missionary work, which aligns with her values and will likely deepen her purpose and drive. Upon her return, she intends to focus completely on launching and growing her wellness business.

Recommendation

I recommende

Zoom no show



zoom

Harry Blecker

Julie has prepared V1 of the valuation that Bonnie needs. JO needs 2025 numbers. I reviewed her reports


Julie Oldham
The request is now complete.

Email/response


I understand. Thank you for your responses. Please use https://calendly.com/southeastmisbdc/phone-call to set a meeting. 


What to Expect

  • We are funded by the Small Business Administration and our services are at no-cost (except some trainings have a small registration fee). 
  • All information we discuss will remain confidential. This information was in the initial documentation that you completed when you requested consulting.
  • Meetings usually last less than 1 hour. Meetings are only by phone and by appointment.
  • We assist both start-up and existing businesses. Some of the topics that we help with are startup steps, business plans, marketing strategies, financial benchmarking, projections and preparing for bank loans.
  • Michigan SBDC does not h

He toured the facility with the realtor. He has also done some side research on value and came up with $360000. I told him about SEV and will look up this number and send it to him. I also told him to reach out to TJ to meet with Sherry. He noted that First Pres was also in the mix for possible funding sources??!! They are located next door

Andy reached out to me to ask about working on his business plan, cash flow, and projections. I followed up to set up a consultation so that we can develop a plan for moving forward.

RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN: I met with Michelle to review her interest in selling her business. She has had some feelers out and some responses to this interest. She needs an Atty and help from Julie O again. She is getting tired of HR issues and needs to begin taking care of family members who are older..in Arizona.

ClientIntake.BusinessType: Real Estate and Rental and Leasing

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Analysis

Adonis has recently made a clear decision to move forward with launching a medical courier business—a promising and in-demand field. As part of his preparations, he successfully opened a business bank account with PNC, marking a strong step in getting his operations off the ground. However, he encountered a significant roadblock when trying to secure business credit cards. Despite having an excellent credit score of 760, he’s been unable to proceed due to an identity verification issue, which is preventing the credit bureaus from fully validating his profile.

This type of challenge can be frustrating, especially when the financial foundation of the business depends on being able to access credit. Still, with the right steps and documentation, it can be resolved relatively quickly.

Recommendation

I recommended that he submit the proper identification and documentation to the major credit bureaus—Experian, Equifax, and

Calendly

Analysis

Natea has recently explored the world of AI and is now feeling energized and inspired by the possibilities it brings to her business. With a newfound understanding of how AI tools can enhance her operations, she’s ready to rethink her direction and take her business to the next level. Previously focused on event planning, she’s now seeking a fresh niche—something that aligns more closely with her evolving interests and the current market. Her goal is to expand beyond events and carve out a new identity for her brand.

This is a pivotal moment for her business, and her excitement about using AI shows that she’s open to innovation and ready for a transformation. The challenge now is identifying the right niche and target audience so she can build a business that reflects her growth and goals.

Recommendation

I recommended that she use AI as a brainstorming partner to explore new niche ideas. Tools like ChatGPT can help h

Our meeting today was brief. They have been working diligently on their website and anticipate having that launched on June 1. their new website is www.little-big–adventures.com


We discussed again the importance of having a liability, waiver or hold harmless agreement for participants. Consultant to see if there is a Michigan base standard hold harmless agreement.


Our next meeting will be June 10 at 11 AM.

1:1 business planning session, prep, BARN notes

I told her that the business plan will help her to gain confidence because it will help her to prove if her concept will work, how to make it work, all of the elements that are needed for success and the resources required. Still in WCC program but has not submitted draft plan. Shared AI version in our meeting that still needs more research and work.

Client in business as a sole proprietor Looking to set up an LLC to expand business, hire employees, and grow. Client looking for capital for a work truck / trailer. Client also looking to set up website. Sent following email:


Rebekah – as discussed – attached is our start up guide. It will give you 1) a step by step guide to setting up your LLC (chapter 3) and 2) hiring your first employee (chapter 6).


Here are a couple of frameworks we use to do a business plan: 1) Lean Canvas 2) Simple Bus Plan one pager. Both will give you an outline type way to approach completing a business plan. High level, the things you want to think about are: Vision for Business, What is ‘Why Buy’ for your business – what makes your business best able to support your target customers, Marketing Strategy – how do you reach your target customer, Market Research on Customer and Competition, any financial projections (revenue, profit and cash generation) including any hi

Met with Jenessa at Ewen Station, the business she and her husband are planning to buy. Current owners Mark and Nancy Voight were present. I was able to get the 2024 income statement and enter information into LivePlan. The asking price is $180,000, of which they are seeking 162,000 in a load through SNB with a down payment of 10% or $18,000. They would not be able to put down 20%. Current owners appear to be very cooperative in helping janessa and her husband to purchase the business. The business has been profitable.

I entered the available info into LivePlan

We discussed that they will need to find a reliable accountant, as they do not have a lot of financial expertise. Bother Janessa and Matt are certified mechanics.

Janessa signed the Client Consent Agreement to share information.


Next steps: I will go over the figues in Lieveplan and adjust them for projections and share the results with Janessa. In our next meeting, we will finalize the b

Reached out to Tawana last week to see if she needed help with anything. As of now, she does not need any assistance but would like to be informed of any grant opportunities. I directed her over to the Flint & Genesee Economic Alliance's website so that she can keep track of their announcements. Closing the case for now.

Summary

Hello,

 

I hope that you are doing well. It has been a while since we connected. I wanted to follow up with you to see if you needed any assistance. How are things going?

 Our goal is to provide you with the latest information that will help you make better decisions, increase sales and improve your competitiveness. As a reminder, we can provide you with consulting and training services on a variety of subjects such as marketing, Search Engine Optimization SEO reports, finance, payment methods as well as in-depth industry or product specific market research and Digital resources. 

 A couple of items that might be of interest to you or someone you know: 

Tariffs

Met with Linda and Tom. The business has moderate sales and is covering costs, but they are finding that the current platform limitations are causing significant challenges and believe that they will need to move to another platform to add new desired features. However, they need to have the transition take place in a way that doesn't impact client access or service. They are thinking of using an agency like Atomic Object to help and expect a cost of between $150-300K to do so, although do not have a proposal they could share. They were hoping for some kind of grant or state funding to cover the cost. I explained that I was not aware of any such funding and the likely the only form would be taking on investment, which they are reticent to do. We discussed their apporach to defining needed changes, etc. and I worked to focus them on continued customer discovery and validation to ensure they new features will have optimal value and drive revenue and market growth. Jeff will work on preparing a set of photos for each product category tonight


Will return tomorrow to make sure he can load them into his website

Jacqui:

 

Not sure if you saw my suggestions to the deck. Let me give you the highlights…

 

  • The good thing is you’ve covered much of the information that MI investors/reviewers consider important.
  • Unclear if this is a presentation deck or a “leave behind deck”

    • If this is a leave behind deck you can be more complete/expansive in the information provided.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Review notes from my write-up last year. Create questions list. email to Tong and Michelle:


Tong and Michelle,

 

I have a couple of questions about the information on this Snapshot:

 

  1. How many years/months will it take to grow the company revenue from $13 Million last year to $30 million?
  2. What ownership percentage are you offering to an investor for the $3 Million investment?
  3. What caused the decline in revenue to $13,056,000 for the year ended June 30 2024 from $15,640,000 for the year ended June 30 2023?
  4. We are 11 months into this fiscal year. How does this year look? Can you share financials YTD with me?

So.. my question wasn't an attack. I'm genuinely asking. Have you ever run a business? Has anybody there run a business?


I ask because I am still struggling to understand what SBDC _DOES_. 


It seems like you offer business 101 level support for people who have no clue what a business plan is, or how to google commercial lenders in Kalamazoo. And if that's what you provide, great, no shade.. but it's not what I need.  


I need somebody I can show my plan to and go over with and look for alternatives and discuss how to grow product lines. I have a business degree (graduated from Haworth) and a basic understanding of the fundamentals. I need mentorship, not "how to first steps&qu

SF Note to SBDC Team Members: Client registered for the SPARK SBSH Program via Greater Washtenaw Region and has been officially accepted by SPARK. The client was referred to our team to participate in a short term business coaching engagement for which the client will be a part of for approximately 3 - 4 months. SPARK opened it to applicants statewide.


The lead SBDC consultant may continue working with the client simultaneously if they wish during this period. Please note that the SBSH operated by the SBSH Business Coaching Team (Millie Chu and T.Rose Malone), training, and coaching operates independently and does not interfere with or require other consultant involvement, as it runs separately from standard SBDC consulting services. Thank you.

Went through the business plan template. She wants to focus on her current retail store then use earnings to get a rentable space for the community to use. Most of her margins are between 40-60% but she is not selling very many items. $6,000 in sales her first month, and $2,000 this month. She wants to use the grant for inventory and maybe some lighting for the shop as it is dark. I will get her some market research and we will meet next week to go over her plan before she submits.

Analysis

Karen has recently taken a new position at a local bookstore, a move she believes will not only provide immediate income but also offer valuable experience in both business and journalism—two fields closely tied to her long-term goals. She has been in communication with an attorney who is quoting her a $4,000 fee, and she is still awaiting further information from a separate attorney related to her patient matters. In the meantime, working at the bookstore allows Karen to immerse herself in a learning environment where she can explore real-world examples of business operations, customer service, and marketing strategies—all while accessing books and resources that support her passion for journalism. Her decision to pursue this job demonstrates initiative, adaptability, and a willingness to build her foundation from the ground up while working toward covering her legal expenses.

Recommendation

It’s recommended that Karen ta

Met with Sadie at her location. She hired a company to help her manage inventory and she hired a new bookkeeper. She has barely be able to keep up and has used a lot of her money that should have gone to inventory to pay bills. The store is in desperate need of more inventory.


We reviewed her P & L and based on her operating expenses and COGS, she is selling well below break even. Recommended increasing inventory, increasing her marketing (with De'Sia's help) and training her staff to upsell. She has credit with Faire.com, so recommended she use that to help increase inventory.


Will check back in 3 weeks.

I spoke briefly with the client today regarding the additional Cohort funds available to the 2024 Cohort participants. She was confused and panicking that the work order for these funds had to be submitted by May 31. I explained to her that it was not the case. Only work orders for the original $7,000 were due, which she has already spent, and she has received her certificate of completion. She was happy to hear this, as she is waiting on a quote from Village Printing in Lapeer to have additional marketing pieces printed for her. She is grateful to know that she has some time to work on this, but also understands that the extra funds are being distributed on a first-come, first-served basis.

Analysis

Kim has taken an exciting first step by signing up for a local farmers market in Grand Ledge. This will be her very first pop-up event, and while she’s feeling a bit nervous, she’s also preparing well. She now has a functional business bank account through Chase, which allows her to accept debit cards, credit cards, and cash—giving her flexibility and professionalism in handling payments on-site.

Visually, she’s already making a strong impression: Kim has a purple-themed setup, including a purple banner and a purple tent that will help her stand out at the market. Her vibrant setup will create a memorable brand presence for first-time shoppers and help attract attention from passersby. However, she is still in the early stages and is figuring out what works, how to set up her booth effectively, and what products will resonate with buyers.

Recommendation

Since this is Kim’s first farmers market experience, I suggested

Garrett is an instructor a SCC. He teaches Engineering & Advanced Manufacturing. His side business is designing and 3D printing items that he sells through Tiktok Shop and Etsy.


He was introduced by Mohamed Adel of SVSU and the intent was marketing, but it seems we need to work on things like an LLC, EIN, etc to get the business paperwork done. We'll also work on a website and other marketing as we go along.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Hi Jasmine,

It was great catching up with you this week and hearing more about the exciting progress you’re making with your barndominium project!

To recap: we reviewed the grant list you provided and discussed your current progress on the business plan — especially the revenue section, which we’ll continue developing together. I really appreciate how you’re thinking strategically about land use, particularly your insight on increasing square footage to drive higher property values, even with fewer acres.

Since you've already been researching cost-per-square-foot comparisons in your area, you’re in a strong position to refine your value proposition. A good tip would be to focus on how design efficiency (layout, multi-use spaces, modular construction) can drive perceived value. A barndominium that lives like a 2,500 sq ft home but is built at a 1,800 sq ft cost could give you a competitive edge.

Also consider highlighting the lifet

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
I spoke with Amonie about how things are going with the business, as it's been a minute since we last spoke over the winter. I just wanted to catch up and hear about their progress so far.

At this point, Amonie is considering dissolving the LLC and starting from scratch with a new entity and new branding. She feels that Luxurious Cleans no longer reflects the business she wants to run, and starting with a new entity will help her restructure the way that she wanted to from the beginning.

To help her with this, Amonie would like help forming a new LLC and EIN, as well as work on a business plan. Her desired name is So Fresh So Pleasant, LLC.

She is also looking for a business address, but is hesitant to use a third party. I recommended checking in to the USPS for a PO Box, and CMURC if a physical address is needed.

Finally, Amonie would like help with re-branding and a web presence. She would like a website that she can manage herself, and is intereste
Worked on payroll setup in QBO. Helped her apply for UIA number and MTO account. Helped her set up 1st payroll. She will verify amounts with accountant, then initiate payroll next week.

Met with Elizabeth, who is preparing for an upcoming meeting with Wendy. We reviewed her cash-on-cash PDF and briefly walked through the basic income/expense spreadsheet. We discussed how to build realistic projections, and I provided a basic overview of how to track and interpret cash flow. Client expressed concern about selecting the right lender and asked what red flags or terms to look out for when evaluating funding options.

Client is interested in doing the projections herself to gain a better understanding of her financial outlook before her next meeting.

Andy’s most recent deliverables were from April 2024 and some things have changed since then so Andy would like to make sure everything is up-to-date. He has paid for a trademark for his brand, as well as all of the necessary licensing, in Michigan but needs guidance on selling/branding in other states. Andy also mentioned wanting to brand his product as “Made in Michigan”. He has looked online but says that sources differ on what exactly makes a product “Made in Michigan”. One of his ingredient suppliers ships from Florida but other than that, everything is sourced from Michigan.

We connect again in 1 month.

Hi Levi!

Thanks—and no worries at all. I appreciate your honesty and your desire to understand what we offer and whether it’s the right fit for where you are in your business journey. No offense was taken, and I value the opportunity to clarify.

You're absolutely right that the MI SBDC frequently supports entrepreneurs in the early stages—those needing foundational help with business planning, financial projections, market research, and general startup navigation. That said, we also regularly work with more established business owners and professionals like yourself who are exploring growth, product development, succession, commercialization, and strategic direction.

You can explore our range of services here:
